preamble: The shift from the voiced velar fricative [ɣ] to the voiceless glottal fricative [h] is a process of debuccalization and devoicing. This weakening involves the loss of the specific velar place of articulation and the cessation of vocal cord vibration, moving the friction to the glottis.

examples.note: Common weakening of the velar fricative in unstressed positions or specific dialectal developments.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Lass, R. (1997). Historical Linguistics and Language Change.
